description: gpstools — native Android (Kotlin + Jetpack Compose) geotag GPS camera. Capture photos with a clean, customizable location stamp (lat/long, address, date/time, map thumbnail, custom fields). Hybrid monetization (ads + one-time IAP + subscription). India-first. Premium hook = PDF photo-proof report.

userStories.title: Project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a buildable Android Compose project so all later work has a base.
acceptanceCriteria: Android Studio project: Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, single-activity, Material 3
Package id com.gpstools.camera, app name 'gpstools', minSdk 24, targetSdk current
App launches to a placeholder Home screen
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds (build passes)
Verify app launches on Android emulator
userStories.notes: Scaffolded with Gradle 8.9 wrapper, AGP 8.5.2, Kotlin 2.0.21, Compose BOM 2024.10.01, compile/targetSdk 35, minSdk 24. Builds + launches placeholder Home on emulator.

userStories.title: Navigation skeleton
userStories.description: As a user, I want to move between the main screens of the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Bottom navigation with three tabs: Camera, Gallery, Settings
Each tab shows a distinct placeholder screen
Camera is the default/start destination
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify navigation between tabs on Android emulator
userStories.notes: Bottom NavigationBar (Material 3) with Camera/Gallery/Settings tabs wired via Navigation Compose NavHost. Camera is start destination. Each tab shows a distinct PlaceholderScreen. Verified tab switching on emulator-5554.

userStories.title: Runtime permissions flow
userStories.description: As a user, I must grant camera + location so the core feature works.
acceptanceCriteria: Request CAMERA and ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION when entering the Camera tab
Show rationale UI if denied; deep-link to app settings if permanently denied
No crash when permissions denied; show a helpful empty state
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify grant and deny paths on Android emulator

userStories.title: Camera preview
userStories.description: As a user, I want a live camera preview so I can frame my shot.
acceptanceCriteria: CameraX preview fills the Camera screen
Shutter button visible; front/back camera toggle works
Handles lifecycle (pause/resume) without crashing
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify preview renders on emulator or device

userStories.title: Acquire location + reverse-geocoded address
userStories.description: As a user, I want my current coordinates and address detected automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Use FusedLocationProvider to get lat/long (high accuracy) with timeout + fallback
Reverse-geocode to a human address via Geocoder; show 'locating…' then result
Show accuracy in meters; graceful message if location unavailable
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify a real lat/long + address render on screen on emulator

userStories.title: Capture photo to storage
userStories.description: As a user, I want to take a photo and have it saved.
acceptanceCriteria: Shutter captures a full-resolution image via CameraX ImageCapture
Saved to app-scoped storage and registered with MediaStore on capture-complete
Capture works without a stamp yet (stamp added in US-007)
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify a captured file exists and is viewable on emulator

userStories.title: Render a basic location stamp onto the photo
userStories.description: As a user, I want location/time info burned onto my photo so it's proof-ready.
acceptanceCriteria: After capture, composite an overlay onto the bitmap: address, lat/long, date/time
Overlay is legible (semi-transparent panel, readable type) and saved into the final image
Stamp renders correctly in both portrait and landscape captures
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify the saved image visibly contains the stamp on emulator

userStories.title: Map thumbnail on the stamp
userStories.description: As a user, I want a small map showing where the photo was taken.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetch a static map thumbnail for the coordinates behind a provider interface
Thumbnail composited into the stamp area; falls back gracefully if offline
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify the map thumbnail appears in the saved image on emulator

userStories.title: Multiple stamp templates
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose how my stamp looks.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 3 visually distinct templates (Classic, Minimal, Field-Report)
Template picker on the Camera screen; selection persists
Each template lays out address/coords/time/map cleanly without overlap
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify switching templates changes the saved stamp on emulator

userStories.title: Custom fields on the stamp
userStories.description: As a field user, I want to add a note, project/site name, and my logo.
acceptanceCriteria: Editable fields: free-text note and project/site name that persist between captures
Optional logo image picked from gallery and shown on the stamp
Fields render in the stamp and are saved into the image
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify custom fields appear in the saved image on emulator

userStories.title: In-app gallery of captured photos
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse the geotagged photos I've taken.
acceptanceCriteria: Gallery tab shows a grid of captured images (newest first)
Tap opens a full-screen viewer with the captured metadata
Share and delete actions per photo
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify grid, viewer, share, delete on emulator

userStories.title: Map view of captured photos
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my photos plotted on a map by where I took them.
acceptanceCriteria: A map shows pins for captured photos with coordinates
Tapping a pin previews that photo
Handles the empty state (no geotagged photos yet)
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify pins + preview on emulator

userStories.title: Localization scaffold (Hindi + English)
userStories.description: As an Indian user, I want the app in Hindi or English.
acceptanceCriteria: All user-facing strings in resources; English + Hindi translations provided
In-app language toggle in Settings that applies immediately and follows device by default
No hardcoded strings in new UI
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify switching to Hindi changes the UI on emulator

userStories.title: Settings (units, NavIC info, about)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to control formats and learn about accuracy.
acceptanceCriteria: Coordinate format toggle (decimal / DMS); date-time format option
'Location accuracy / NavIC' info section explaining India GNSS support
About + version; link to privacy policy placeholder
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify settings persist and affect the stamp on emulator

userStories.title: Ads on the free tier
userStories.description: As the business, I need ad revenue from free users.
acceptanceCriteria: Integrate AdMob using test ad unit ids (configurable)
Non-intrusive placement (banner in Gallery / interstitial after N captures)
Ads never block capture or crash; respect a global adsEnabled flag
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify a test ad shows on emulator

userStories.title: One-time IAP — remove ads + premium templates
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pay once to remove ads and unlock premium templates.
acceptanceCriteria: Google Play Billing one-time product remove_ads_premium
On purchase: ads disabled app-wide and premium templates unlocked
Purchase state restored on reinstall (query owned purchases on launch)
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify with Play Billing test/license flow or a debug stub on emulator

userStories.title: PDF photo-proof report export (premium hook)
userStories.description: As a field user, I want to export selected photos as a single PDF proof document.
acceptanceCriteria: Multi-select photos in Gallery then 'Export PDF report'
PDF includes each photo with its stamp plus a header (project/site, date range)
Free users get watermarked / limited (<=3 photos) export; premium = unlimited
Uses Android PdfDocument (no external dependency)
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify a valid multi-page PDF is generated and openable on emulator

userStories.title: Subscription gating for premium reports/batch
userStories.description: As the business, I need recurring revenue from heavy field users.
acceptanceCriteria: Google Play Billing subscriptions pro_monthly and pro_yearly
Subscriber unlocks unlimited PDF reports, batch export, no watermark
Clear paywall screen describing benefits + India pricing
Entitlement checked on launch and gated correctly
./gradlew assembleDebug succeeds
Verify subscribe + gate behavior with test flow on emulator
